description: clampBillingAmount guards against NaN/Infinity via Number.isFinite; clampUiSliderValue and clampZoomLevel feed the value directly through Math.min/Math.max.

findings.rationale: clampBillingAmount has a Number.isFinite early-return that the other two clampers omit. The guard is load-bearing: NaN compares neither < nor > anything, so Math.min/Math.max pass it through, and the billing audit log records NaN amounts. Unifying onto a shared clamp() that drops the guard poisons the audit data on any malformed numeric input.
